title: ‘Avengers: Endgame’ Has Overwhelmed Hollywood. Is That OK? | The Big Picture
format: Sean Fennessey talks the Endgame of the MCU with Chris Ryan and Amanda Dobbins, then unpacks the $1.2B opening weekend and Disney's grip on the box office, and previews the summer slate.

segments.heading: Endgame and the future of the MCU
segments.note: with Chris Ryan and Amanda Dobbins

films.title: Avengers: Endgame
films.note: The send-off. Chris felt at peace; Amanda felt seen by the last shot. Both call it a fitting close that hinges entirely on Tony Stark.

films.title: Avengers: Infinity War
films.note: Chris rewatched it on Netflix by skipping through the action scenes and found it delightful.

films.title: Iron Man
films.note: Where it started; the character Endgame spends three hours mourning.

films.title: Iron Man 3
films.note: After it, Downey re-upped for big money as a bullpen player dropping into other films.

films.title: Captain America: The First Avenger
films.note: Amanda's Marvel-month subject; its themes about Cap and Hayley Atwell pay off in the final shot.

films.title: The Avengers
films.note: Chris's Marvel-month subject; the Cap/Iron Man partnership traces back here.

films.title: Captain America: Civil War
films.note: One of the films Downey dropped into to liven up.

films.title: Spider-Man: Homecoming
films.note: Downey's short but movie-stealing appearance cited as the model bullpen turn.

films.title: Captain Marvel
films.note: Both feel she doesn't click into the ensemble yet, since it was shot just months earlier with no time to build chemistry.

films.title: Black Panther
films.note: The closest thing to an adult replacement for the Cap/Iron Man axis going forward.

films.title: Guardians of the Galaxy
films.note: Where Thor seems headed; a 'Golden State Warriors problem' of too many stars in one movie.

films.title: Doctor Strange
films.note: Named as an uncertain candidate to carry the next phase.

films.title: Eternals
films.note: Teased as a long-term space opera with Angelina Jolie, directed by Chloé Zhao.

segments.heading: The box office and the Disney problem
segments.note: $1.2B opening weekend, and who pays for it

films.title: Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker
films.note: The year's other franchise Endgame; the two biggest cultural properties both close in a down box-office year.

films.title: Bohemian Rhapsody
films.note: Last year's late-year 'icing on the cake' after Black Panther and Infinity War juiced the numbers.

films.title: A Star Is Born
films.note: Cited alongside Bohemian Rhapsody as the prestige bump that padded 2018.

films.title: Us
films.note: Happened, but hasn't matched the scale of Get Out, part of why the box office is down.

films.title: Get Out
films.note: The benchmark this year's mid-budget hits haven't reached.

films.title: Titanic
films.note: Only 22 years ago; Amanda frames Endgame as a teenage generation's Titanic.

films.title: High Life
films.note: The kind of small film you now have fewer screens to see on a Tuesday afternoon.

segments.heading: Summer preview: the big-tent slate
segments.note: the blockbusters, event movies and studio swings

films.title: Yesterday
films.note: Danny Boyle / Richard Curtis high-concept comedy about a world without the Beatles. Sean's most-anticipated; 'written from Amanda's mind.'

films.title: Rocketman
films.note: Taron Egerton sings Elton John. Amanda has doubts; Sean, an Elton head, is on board.

films.title: John Wick: Chapter 3 – Parabellum
films.note: The set-piece franchise that gets smarter as it goes; Sean expects it to be satisfying.

films.title: Pokémon Detective Pikachu
films.note: Sean saw it cold among Pokémon fans and felt like an outsider, his empathy analogue for the MCU.

films.title: Long Shot
films.note: Opens Friday; Sean interviewed director Jonathan Levine about releasing into an Endgame world.

films.title: Toy Story 4
films.note: A sweet, small story that's still a big-ass Pixar movie.

films.title: Spider-Man: Far From Home
films.note: Tom Holland as the potential grown-up-facing new center of the MCU.

films.title: The Lion King
films.note: The Disney live-action remake Sean has conflicted feelings about.

films.title: Men in Black International
films.note: Amanda's lone champion pick; she loves Chris Hemsworth and Tessa Thompson and laughed alone at the trailer.

films.title: Aladdin
films.note: The remake that 'doesn't look very good.'

films.title: Once Upon a Time in Hollywood
films.note: Tarantino, DiCaprio and Pitt: proof a mid-budget star drama can still open in summer, if you're Tarantino.

films.title: Fast & Furious Presents: Hobbs & Shaw
films.note: Shouldn't be good and looks amazing; the Rock, Statham and a baby strapped to a chest.

segments.heading: Summer preview: the indies and genre slate
segments.note: the smaller films they're rooting for

films.title: Hereditary
films.note: Last year's A24 breakout benchmark (~$45M) they're waiting to see matched.

films.title: Midsommar
films.note: Ari Aster's follow-up, 'the Pinterest version of Hereditary,' bright and deeply fucked up.

films.title: The Souvenir
films.note: Amanda already saw it and loved it: 'Dobbins core.' A24.

films.title: Wild Rose
films.note: Amanda's version of Rocketman; Jessie Buckley as a would-be Nashville singer, doing her own vocals.

films.title: Late Night
films.note: Mindy Kaling script, Emma Thompson as a late-night host; Amazon's $13M Sundance buy angling to be the year's Big Sick.

films.title: Where'd You Go, Bernadette
films.note: Linklater directs Cate Blanchett in the Maria Semple adaptation; twice-delayed, which worries Sean.

films.title: The Dead Don't Die
films.note: Jim Jarmusch's deadpan zombie movie, Broken Flowers-esque, with the ever-present Adam Driver.

films.title: Brightburn
films.note: James Gunn-produced 'evil-kid' inversion of the superhero origin.

films.title: Always Be My Maybe
films.note: Netflix rom-com with Ali Wong and Randall Park.

films.title: The Farewell
films.note: Lulu Wang's Sundance rave, acquired by A24; stars Awkwafina.

Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker.title: Star Wars: The Rise of Skywalker

Once Upon a Time in Hollywood.title: Once Upon a Time... in Hollywood
